titleStmt.title: Theo (Hinrich Gustav) Goos
born Neumünster (Holstein), June 3, 1906; died ibid, January 1, 1978

publicationStmt.publisher: Schenker Documents Online in association with the Centre for Computing in the Humanities

sourceDesc.p: No source: created in electronic format.

encodingDesc.p: Encoding according to the CCH TEI Guidelines

div.p: Student of Reinhard Oppel at the Leipzig Conservatory.

div.head: Life
div.p: Goos’s parents were Friedrich Goos and Katharine (née Tischler) of Neumünster (Holstein). Nothing is known of his early life other than that he attended elementary school (Volksschule) rather than the more advanced Gymnasium. He was admitted to the Leipzig Conservatory c. 1931 as a pianist. Oppel wrote extensively about him to Schenker in 1931 and 1933, but his letters are silent on him thereafter. Goos lived and died in Neumünster, and nothing is known of his career.

div.head: Goos and Schenker
div.p: As a student of Reinhard Oppel, an early interest in Schenker’s theory led him to write to the master inquiring about the study of his theory, for which Oppel scolded him, and to which Schenker replied outlining his standard tuition plan but remarking that it was “not adaptable to schools with diploma candidates” (diary, December 14, 1931). Two years later Oppel reported that Goos had made an “excellent analysis” of the Prelude to the Bach C major Cello Suite and had also compiled a list of “Schenker words” on which he was seeking to give a radio talk (OJ 13/10, [166]).
